hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 binding. A negatively charged residue at position 6 may disrupt off-target electrostatic complementarity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore and cysteine residues. Iteration 6 confirms the need for focused mutagenesis at positions 1/2/5/6/11/12 to achieve this.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing on these positions will allow for targeted mutagenesis to disrupt off-target binding.

Introducing mutations at these positions with residues that can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore region (positions 7-10) and cysteine residues (Cys3/Cys14) will improve selectivity.
